---
title: "Lab Notebook"
listing:
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: default
  categories: true
  sort-ui: true
  filter-ui: true
  fields: [date, title, reading-time, description, categories]
  date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
  feed: true
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
---

## Lab Notebook

This is an open record of ongoing research exploring how foraminifera respond to environmental stress in natural marine systems. Entries document experimental design, field sampling, protocol development, and early-stage results.

Rather than only presenting final conclusions, this notebook captures the **process of science in real time**—including challenges, adjustments, and unexpected observations.

---

## What This Notebook Tracks

- Field sampling across tidal cycles and environmental conditions  
- Development of in situ glyoxal fixation protocols  
- Microscopy and morphospecies identification  
- RNA extraction and sequencing preparation  
- Data exploration and early interpretations  

---

## How to Use This Page

- **Search** to find specific experiments, methods, or observations  
- **Filter by category** (e.g., sampling, microscopy, RNA-seq)  
- **Sort entries** by date or topic  
- **Browse chronologically** to follow the progression of the project  

---

## Current Project Focus

The current phase of research is centered on:

- Optimizing **glyoxal fixation** for field conditions  
- Comparing **in situ vs lab-preserved samples**  
- Capturing short-term biological responses across **tidal changes**  

---

## Why an Open Lab Notebook?

Maintaining an open notebook increases transparency and reproducibility in scientific research. It also provides a clearer picture of how knowledge is built—from initial questions to refined methods and final results.

---
